title: International Regulations Assessment
description: Assessment covering EU directives, UK regulations, Canadian requirements, and international standards harmonization.

questions.question: Which EU Directive governs worker exposure to artificial optical radiation?
questions.explanation: EU Directive 2006/25/EC on the minimum health and safety requirements regarding the exposure of workers to risks arising from physical agents (artificial optical radiation) applies to laser workers.

questions.question: What replaced CE marking in the UK domestic market after Brexit?
questions.explanation: UKCA (UK Conformity Assessed) marking replaced CE marking for products placed on the UK domestic market after Brexit. CE marking may still be required for products sold in the EU.

questions.question: Canada regulates laser products under which legislation?
questions.explanation: Health Canada regulates laser products under the Radiation Emitting Devices Act (REDA), which sets performance standards for laser products sold in Canada.

questions.question: Which IEC technical committee is responsible for laser safety standards?
questions.explanation: IEC Technical Committee 76 (TC76) is responsible for laser safety standards, including IEC 60825 series. TC76 works with ISO and national standards bodies on harmonization.

questions.question: AS/NZS 2211 is the laser safety standard for which region?
questions.explanation: AS/NZS 2211 is the joint Australian/New Zealand laser safety standard series, which adopts IEC 60825 with Australasian-specific modifications.

questions.question: Which edition of IEC 60825-1 is the current international baseline?
questions.explanation: IEC 60825-1 Edition 3 (2014) is the current edition forming the basis of international harmonization. It introduced changes to the classification system and accessible emission limits.
